tldr: Bugsnag is a hosted error monitoring product; Crashpad is a low-level open-source crash capture library. Replacing one with the other means giving up the dashboard, alerts, and grouping in exchange for a self-built or third-party backend.
what_to_export: Stability targets and release tracking config
export_method: Document Bugsnag projects, environments, members, alerts, and integrations from the Bugsnag UI or its API. Historical events generally do not migrate to a self-hosted setup; record the issues you actively track and any aggregates you rely on. Save symbol upload steps as build-pipeline code. Treat tokens and API keys as values to rotate after cutover. Where Bugsnags API does not clearly expose a setting, document it manually rather than assuming an endpoint.
import_method: Crashpad is a library, not a service, so the import is really an architectural change. Integrate Crashpad into your native applications following its official documentation, replacing Bugsnags native SDK initialization. Stand up a backend that can receive minidumps from Crashpads HTTP uploader; many teams pair Crashpad with Sentry, Backtrace, or a custom service rather than building from scratch. For non-native applications that previously used Bugsnag, choose another error monitoring tool because Crashpad does not address them. Wire symbol upload to your chosen backend, configure deploy markers, and validate with controlled crashes before retiring Bugsnag.

concept_mapping.target: Provided by chosen backend, not Crashpad

gotchas: Crashpad provides capture only; you still need a backend
No drop-in replacement for non-native Bugsnag SDKs
Dashboards, grouping, and alerting must come from the backend you choose
Symbol management, retention, and access control become your responsibility
Historical Bugsnag data does not transfer

email.subject: Winding down our Bugsnag usage
email.body:
Hi team,

We are moving our native crash capture to an open-source library and operating the backend ourselves, so we plan to stop using Bugsnag. Our target timeline is 14 days from today.

During that period we will export project and integration configuration, document alert rules, and run the new pipeline in parallel for validation. After cutover, our applications will stop sending events to Bugsnag.

Could you confirm the steps to disable auto-renewal, the effective end date under our current commitment, and any final invoicing details? Please share any recommended actions before account deactivation.

Thanks for the support.

Your customer
